revision.comment: [[静岡県]][[磐田市]]の[[神社]]。[[遠江国]]の二宮
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=鹿苑神社| 画像=| 所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮 1767| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|31|N|137|51|30|E}}| 祭神=[[大国主|大名牟遅命]]| 社格=式内社（小）・[[郷社]]| 創建=伝・[[履中天皇]]4年（[[403年]]）| 本殿=[[八幡造]]銅板葺| 例祭=10月第1日曜日 }} '''鹿苑神社'''（ろくおんじんじゃ）は[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]の[[神社]]。北側は連福寺と接しており、[[磐田駅]]の東南東、[[横須賀街道]]の南方にある。[[二宮神社 (新居町)|二宮神社]] とともに[[遠江国]]の二宮であり、[[江戸時代]]末までは'''高根明神社'''と称し、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|高彦根命]]を祀っていた。
==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[磐田郡]]の小社・鹿神社に比定される。[[遠江国]]の鹿苑神は[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）に[[従五位下]]に叙せられ<ref>『[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]』 [[嘉祥]]3年[[7月11日_(旧暦)|7月11日]]（[[850年]][[8月25日]]）条</ref>、[[貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）には従四位下が授けられたという<ref>『[[日本三代実録|三代実録]]』 [[貞観]]2年[[1月27日_(旧暦)|正月27日]]（[[860年]][[2月26日]]）条</ref>。かつては[[山香郡]]与利郷（現・[[浜松市]][[天竜区]][[春野町 (静岡県)|春野町]]）の正国六音大菩薩社の位置にあり<ref>『遠江国風土記伝』</ref>、[[元慶]]5年（[[881年]]）に磐田郡を分割して山香郡が設置されたのにともない、遠江の[[国府]]近くに移したとされる。
[[鎌倉時代]]には二宮と呼ばれるようになり、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）頃の「遠江国二宮領」という鹿苑神社領に関する記述が残っている<ref>『基重言上状』</ref>。[[江戸時代]]には二之宮村など14ヵ村の[[総鎮守]]であり、[[元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に[[社殿]]を再建した[[棟札]]が残る。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には社領23[[石_(単位)|石]]を寄進された。
==アクセス== *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]][[東海道本線]][[磐田駅]]から徒歩10分
==参考文献== *日本歴史地名大系（オンライン版） 小学館 （『日本歴史地名大系』 平凡社、1979年-2002年 を基にしたデータベース） <references />
==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/toutoumi/bun/to120702-01.html 鹿苑神社]（延喜式神社の調査）
{{DEFAULTSORT:ろくおんしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]]

{{神社| 名称=鹿苑神社| 画像=| 所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮 1767| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|31|N|137|51|30|E}}| 祭神=[[大国主|大名牟遅命]]| 社格=式内社（小）・[[郷社]]| 創建=伝・[[履中天皇]]4年（[[403年]]）| 本殿=[[八幡造]]銅板葺| 例祭=10月第1日曜日 }} '''鹿苑神社'''（ろくおんじんじゃ）は[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]の[[神社]]。北側は連福寺と接しており、[[磐田駅]]の東南東、[[横須賀街道]]の南方にある。[[二宮神社 (新居町)|二宮神社]] とともに[[遠江国]]の二宮であり、[[江戸時代]]末までは'''高根明神社'''と称し、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|高彦根命]]を祀っていた。
==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[磐田郡]]の小社・鹿神社に比定される。[[遠江国]]の鹿苑神は[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）に[[従五位下]]に叙せられ<ref>『[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]』 [[嘉祥]]3年[[7月11日_(旧暦)|7月11日]]（[[850年]][[8月25日]]）条</ref>、[[貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）には従四位下が授けられたという<ref>『[[日本三代実録|三代実録]]』 [[貞観]]2年[[1月27日_(旧暦)|正月27日]]（[[860年]][[2月26日]]）条</ref>。かつては[[山香郡]]与利郷（現・[[浜松市]][[天竜区]][[春野町 (静岡県)|春野町]]）の正国六音大菩薩社の位置にあり<ref>『遠江国風土記伝』</ref>、[[元慶]]5年（[[881年]]）に磐田郡を分割して山香郡が設置されたのにともない、遠江の[[国府]]近くに移したとされる。
[[鎌倉時代]]には二宮と呼ばれるようになり、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）頃の「遠江国二宮領」という鹿苑神社領に関する記述が残っている<ref>『基重言上状』</ref>。[[江戸時代]]には二之宮村など14ヵ村の[[総鎮守]]であり、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に[[社殿]]を再建した[[棟札]]が残る。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には社領23[[石_(単位)|石]]を寄進された。
==アクセス== *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]][[東海道本線]][[磐田駅]]から徒歩10分
==参考文献== *日本歴史地名大系（オンライン版） 小学館 （『日本歴史地名大系』 平凡社、1979年-2002年 を基にしたデータベース） <references />
==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/toutoumi/bun/to120702-01.html 鹿苑神社]（延喜式神社の調査）
{{DEFAULTSORT:ろくおんしんしや}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]]

{{神社| 名称=鹿苑神社| 画像=| 所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮 1767| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|31|N|137|51|30|E}}| 祭神=[[大国主|大名牟遅命]]| 社格=式内社（小）・[[郷社]]| 創建=伝・[[履中天皇]]4年（[[403年]]）| 本殿=[[八幡造]]銅板葺| 例祭=10月第1日曜日 }} '''鹿苑神社'''（ろくおんじんじゃ）は[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]の[[神社]]。北側は連福寺と接しており、[[磐田駅]]の東南東、[[横須賀街道]]の南方にある。[[二宮神社 (新居町)|二宮神社]] とともに[[遠江国]]の二宮であり、[[江戸時代]]末までは'''高根明神社'''と称し、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|高彦根命]]を祀っていた。
==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[磐田郡]]の小社・鹿神社に比定される。[[遠江国]]の鹿苑神は[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）に[[従五位下]]に叙せられ<ref>『[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]』 [[嘉祥]]3年[[7月11日_(旧暦)|7月11日]]（[[850年]][[8月25日]]）条</ref>、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）には従四位下が授けられたという<ref>『[[日本三代実録|三代実録]]』 貞観2年[[1月27日_(旧暦)|正月27日]]（[[860年]][[2月26日]]）条</ref>。かつては[[山香郡]]与利郷（現・[[浜松市]][[天竜区]][[春野町 (静岡県)|春野町]]）の正国六音大菩薩社の位置にあり<ref>『遠江国風土記伝』</ref>、[[元慶]]5年（[[881年]]）に磐田郡を分割して山香郡が設置されたのにともない、遠江の[[国府]]近くに移したとされる。
[[鎌倉時代]]には二宮と呼ばれるようになり、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）頃の「遠江国二宮領」という鹿苑神社領に関する記述が残っている<ref>『基重言上状』</ref>。[[江戸時代]]には二之宮村など14ヵ村の[[総鎮守]]であり、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に[[社殿]]を再建した[[棟札]]が残る。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には社領23[[石_(単位)|石]]を寄進された。
==アクセス== *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]][[東海道本線]][[磐田駅]]から徒歩10分
==参考文献== *日本歴史地名大系（オンライン版） 小学館 （『日本歴史地名大系』 平凡社、1979年-2002年 を基にしたデータベース） <references />

{{神社| 名称=鹿苑神社| 画像=| 所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮 1767| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|31|N|137|51|30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=[[大国主|大名牟遅命]]| 社格=式内社（小）・[[郷社]]| 創建=伝・[[履中天皇]]4年（[[403年]]）| 本殿=[[八幡造]]銅板葺| 例祭=10月第1日曜日 }} '''鹿苑神社'''（ろくおんじんじゃ）は[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]の[[神社]]。北側は連福寺と接しており、[[磐田駅]]の東南東、[[横須賀街道]]の南方にある。神紋は「十四菊」である。[[二宮神社 (新居町)|二宮神社]] とともに[[遠江国]]の二宮であり、[[江戸時代]]末までは'''高根明神社'''と称し、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|高彦根命]]を祀っていた。
==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[磐田郡]]の小社・鹿神社に比定される。[[遠江国]]の鹿苑神は[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）に[[従五位下]]に叙せられ<ref>『[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]』 [[嘉祥]]3年[[7月11日_(旧暦)|7月11日]]（[[850年]][[8月25日]]）条</ref>、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）には従四位下が授けられたという<ref>『[[日本三代実録|三代実録]]』 貞観2年[[1月27日_(旧暦)|正月27日]]（[[860年]][[2月26日]]）条</ref>。かつては[[山香郡]]与利郷（現・[[浜松市]][[天竜区]][[春野町 (静岡県)|春野町]]）の正国六音大菩薩社の位置にあり<ref>『遠江国風土記伝』</ref>、[[元慶]]5年（[[881年]]）に磐田郡を分割して山香郡が設置されたのにともない、遠江の[[国府]]近くに移したとされる。
[[鎌倉時代]]には二宮と呼ばれるようになり、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）頃の「遠江国二宮領」という鹿苑神社領に関する記述が残っている<ref>『基重言上状』</ref>。[[江戸時代]]には二之宮村など14ヵ村の[[総鎮守]]であり、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に[[社殿]]を再建した[[棟札]]が残る。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には社領23[[石_(単位)|石]]を寄進された。
==アクセス== *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]][[東海道本線]][[磐田駅]]から徒歩10分
==参考文献== *日本歴史地名大系（オンライン版） 小学館 （『日本歴史地名大系』 平凡社、1979年-2002年 を基にしたデータベース） <references /> {{神道 横}}

[[鎌倉時代]]には二宮と呼ばれるようになり、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）頃の「遠江国二宮領」という鹿苑神社領に関する記述が残っている<ref>『基重言上状』</ref>。[[江戸時代]]には二之宮村など14ヵ村の[[総鎮守]]であり、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に[[社殿]]を再建した[[棟札]]が残る。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には社領23[[石_(単位)|石]]を寄進された。

{{神社| 名称=鹿苑神社| 画像=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
拝殿| 所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮1767| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|31|N|137|51|30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=[[大国主|大名牟遅命]]| 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
[[遠江国]][[一宮|二宮]]
旧[[郷社]]| 創建=（伝）第17代[[履中天皇]]4年| 本殿=[[八幡造]]銅板葺| 例祭=10月第1日曜日 }} '''鹿苑神社'''（ろくおんじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]、[[遠江国]][[一宮|二宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 北側は連福寺と接しており、[[磐田駅]]の東南東、[[横須賀街道]]の南方にある。神紋は「十四菊」である。[[江戸時代]]末までは「高根明神社」と称し、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|高彦根命]]を祀っていた。 ==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[磐田郡]]の小社・鹿神社に比定される。[[遠江国]]の鹿苑神は[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）に[[従五位下]]に叙せられ『[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]』 [[嘉祥]]3年[[7月11日_(旧暦)|7月11日]]（[[850年]][[8月25日]]）条、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）には従四位下が授けられたという『[[日本三代実録|三代実録]]』 貞観2年[[1月27日_(旧暦)|正月27日]]（[[860年]][[2月26日]]）条。かつては[[山香郡]]与利郷（現・[[浜松市]][[天竜区]][[春野町 (静岡県)|春野町]]）の正国六音大菩薩社の位置にあり『遠江国風土記伝』、[[元慶]]5年（[[881年]]）に磐田郡を分割して山香郡が設置されたのにともない、遠江の[[国府]]近くに移したとされる。 [[鎌倉時代]]には二宮と呼ばれるようになり、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）頃の「遠江国二宮領」という鹿苑神社領に関する記述が残っている『基重言上状』。[[江戸時代]]には二之宮村など14ヵ村の[[総鎮守]]であり、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に[[社殿]]を再建した[[棟札]]が残る。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には社領23[[石_(単位)|石]]を寄進された。 == 境内 == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|境内全景 File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|境内鳥居 File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 ==交通アクセス== *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]][[東海道本線]][[磐田駅]]から徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} ==参考文献== *日本歴史地名大系（オンライン版） 小学館　（『日本歴史地名大系』 平凡社、1979年-2002年 を基にしたデータベース） == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ろくおんしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:遠江国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 内容修正 神紋「十四菊」→「十六菊」
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=鹿苑神社 | 画像=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮1767 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|42|31|N|137|51|30|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[大国主|大名牟遅命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
[[遠江国]][[一宮|二宮]]
旧[[郷社]] | 創建=（伝）第17代[[履中天皇]]4年 | 本殿=[[八幡造]]銅板葺 | 例祭=10月第1日曜日 }} '''鹿苑神社'''（ろくおんじんじゃ）は、[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]、[[遠江国]][[一宮|二宮]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 北側は連福寺と接しており、[[磐田駅]]の東南東、[[横須賀街道]]の南方にある。神紋は「十六菊」である。[[江戸時代]]末までは「高根明神社」と称し、[[アヂスキタカヒコネ|高彦根命]]を祀っていた。 ==歴史== [[延喜式神名帳]]にある[[磐田郡]]の小社・鹿神社に比定される。[[遠江国]]の鹿苑神は[[嘉祥]]3年（[[850年]]）に[[従五位下]]に叙せられ『[[日本文徳天皇実録|文徳実録]]』 [[嘉祥]]3年[[7月11日_(旧暦)|7月11日]]（[[850年]][[8月25日]]）条、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]2年（[[860年]]）には従四位下が授けられたという『[[日本三代実録|三代実録]]』 貞観2年[[1月27日_(旧暦)|正月27日]]（[[860年]][[2月26日]]）条。かつては[[山香郡]]与利郷（現・[[浜松市]][[天竜区]][[春野町 (静岡県)|春野町]]）の正国六音大菩薩社の位置にあり『遠江国風土記伝』、[[元慶]]5年（[[881年]]）に磐田郡を分割して山香郡が設置されたのにともない、遠江の[[国府]]近くに移したとされる。 [[鎌倉時代]]には二宮と呼ばれるようになり、[[文永]]5年（[[1268年]]）頃の「遠江国二宮領」という鹿苑神社領に関する記述が残っている『基重言上状』。[[江戸時代]]には二之宮村など14ヵ村の[[総鎮守]]であり、[[元和 (日本)|元和]]9年（[[1623年]]）に[[社殿]]を再建した[[棟札]]が残る。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には社領23[[石_(単位)|石]]を寄進された。 == 境内 == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|境内全景 File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|本殿 File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|境内社 File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|境内鳥居 File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|鳥居 ==交通アクセス== *[[東海旅客鉄道|JR東海]][[東海道本線]][[磐田駅]]から徒歩10分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} ==参考文献== *日本歴史地名大系（オンライン版） 小学館　（『日本歴史地名大系』 平凡社、1979年-2002年 を基にしたデータベース） == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ろくおんしんしや}} [[Category:静岡県の神社]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:遠江国の式内社]] [[Category:静岡県の旧郷社]] [[Category:遠江国|社2]] [[Category:二宮]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 | 名称=鹿苑神社 | 画像=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[静岡県]][[磐田市]]二ノ宮1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=[[大国主|大名牟遅命]] | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
[[遠江国]][[一宮|二宮]]

revision.comment: Marking a third subcategory, unfortunately there will probably be overlap with the second category, but I think that is probably worth it, no harm unless a cumulative thousand are in the overlap
revision.text:

[[Category:hh]][[Category:#redirect[[en:Rokuon Shrine]][[Category:hh]][[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]]]

{{神社 | 名称=鹿苑神社 | 画像=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | 所在地=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4年 | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造}}銅板葺 | 例祭=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}』 嘉祥3年{{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日}}（850年8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』 貞観2年{{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日}}（860年2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[ja:鹿苑神社]]

{{神社 | 名称=鹿苑神社 | 画像=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]

{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮}}

During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:鹿苑神社]]

In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:dd]][[ja:鹿苑神社]]

{{神社 | 名称=鹿苑神社 | 画像=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | 所在地=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto}} | 社格={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4年 | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造}}銅板葺 | 例祭=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}』 嘉祥3年{{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日}}（850年8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』 貞観2年{{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日}}（860年2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[ja:鹿苑神社]]

旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4年 | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}』 嘉祥3年{{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日}}（850年8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』 貞観2年{{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日}}（860年2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Rokuon Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4年 | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} under the old shrine ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}』 嘉祥3年{{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日}}（850年8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』 貞観2年{{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日}}（860年2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Rokuon Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rokuon Shrine}}{{citation needed|Rokuon Shrine}}

The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Rokuon Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rokuon Shrine}}{{citation needed|Rokuon Shrine}}

In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|12=draft}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Rokuon Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rokuon Shrine}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|12=draft}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮|12=draft}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造|12=draft}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the old shrine ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=draft}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥|12=draft}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録|12=draft}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日|12=draft}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|12=draft}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区|12=draft}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=draft}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶|12=draft}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永|12=draft}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和|12=draft}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=draft}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|12=draft}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Rokuon Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rokuon Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|12=draft}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|12=draft}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮|12=draft}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造|12=draft}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} under the old shrine ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|12=draft}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=draft}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥|12=draft}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録|12=draft}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日|12=draft}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|12=draft}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|12=draft}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区|12=draft}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=draft}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶|12=draft}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永|12=draft}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和|12=draft}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=draft}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|12=draft}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|12=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Rokuon Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Rokuon Shrine}} [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]]

In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya (Shinto)|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-zukuri|qq=}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashō (Japan)|qq=}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7月11日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-ku|qq=}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gangyō era|qq=}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei era|qq=}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna (Japan)|qq=}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keian era|qq=}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]]

In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keian era|qq=}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|lt=Ōnamuchi no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|ja|遠江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōtōmi Province|qq=}} {{ill|Ninomiya (Shinto)|ja|二宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya (Shinto)|qq=}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|ja|八幡造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman-zukuri|qq=}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|アヂスキタカヒコネ|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|qq=}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō (Japan)|ja|嘉祥|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashō (Japan)|qq=}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|ja|日本文徳天皇実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qq=}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|7月11日 (旧暦)|ja|7月11日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/7月11日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Jōgan (Japan)|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan (Japan)|qq=}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|ja|天竜区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenryū-ku|qq=}}, [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō era|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gangyō era|qq=}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bunei era|ja|文永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunei era|qq=}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Genna (Japan)|ja|元和|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna (Japan)|qq=}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian era|ja|慶安|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keian era|qq=}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|ja|東海道本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Main Line|qq=}} operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Central|qq=}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]]

[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:磐田市の建築物]]

[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:磐田市の建築物]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]]

In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:磐田市の建築物]] [[Category:磐田市の歴史]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]]
