{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|qq=}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:磐田市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]]

Until the late [[Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:二宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=[[File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=[[:en:Ōkuninushi|Ōnamuchi no Mikoto]] | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}}

Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:Rokuon Shrine]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|qid=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|qid=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|qid=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|qid=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}}

In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677253]] Wikidata name: Rokuon-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}}

It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677253]] Wikidata name: Rokuon-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677253]] Wikidata name: Rokuon-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}}
