旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}} {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677253]] Wikidata name: Rokuon-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677253]] Wikidata name: Rokuon-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location=[[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka]] [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]] Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
[[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]] [[:en:Ninomiya|Ninomiya (Shinto)]]
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. 旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿=[[:en:Hachiman-zukuri|Hachiman-zukuri]]銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Ninomiya, [[:en:Iwata|Iwata]], [[:en:Shizuoka Prefecture|Shizuoka Prefecture]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of [[:en:Tōtōmi Province|Tōtōmi Province]], and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined [[:en:Ajisukitakahikone|Takahikone no Mikoto]]. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in [[:en:Kashō|Kashō (Japan)]] 3 (850 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku]]』 嘉祥3rd year [[:en:July 11|7月11日 (旧暦)]]（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan (Japan)]] 2 (860 CE)『[[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, [[:en:Tenryū-ku|Tenryū-ku]], [[:en:Hamamatsu|Hamamatsu]]), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the [[:en:Gangyō|Gangyō era]] 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from [[:en:Bun'ei|Bunei era]] 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna (Japan)]] (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of [[:en:Keian|Keian era]] (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from [[:en:Iwata Station|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]] operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677253]] Wikidata name: Rokuon-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location={{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka}} {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}} Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}} {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|WD=Q841389|lt=Ninomiya (Shinto)}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677253]] Wikidata name: Rokuon-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location={{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka}} {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}} Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}} {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|WD=Q841389|lt=Ninomiya (Shinto)}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Qq]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location={{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka}} {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}} Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}} {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|WD=Q841389|lt=Ninomiya (Shinto)}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location={{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka}} {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}} Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}} {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|WD=Q841389|lt=Ninomiya (Shinto)}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District.
