It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku|1=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era|1=Gangyō}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)|1=Yuanhe}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location={{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata}} Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}} {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|WD=Q841389|lt=Ninomiya (Shinto)|1=Ninomiya}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)|1=Kashō}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)|1=July 11}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|1=Zhenguan}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku|1=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era|1=Gangyō}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)|1=Yuanhe}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku|1=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)|1=Kashō}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)|1=July 11}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|1=Zhenguan}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku|1=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era|1=Gangyō}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)|1=Yuanhe}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)|1=Kashō}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)|1=July 11}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|1=Zhenguan}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku|1=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era|1=Gangyō}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)|1=Yuanhe}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ninomiya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)|1=Kashō}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)|1=July 11}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|1=Zhenguan}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku|1=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era|1=Gangyō}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)|1=Yuanhe}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Rokuon-jinja]][[ja:鹿苑神社]] [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=鹿苑神社 | image=File:Rokuon-jinja, haiden-1.jpg | location={{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata}} Ninomiya 1767 | 位置={{Coord|34|42|31.9|N|137|51|30.9|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）
{{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}} {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|WD=Q841389|lt=Ninomiya (Shinto)|1=Ninomiya}}
旧{{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | 創建=(伝) [[履中天皇|Emperor Richū]] 4th year | 本殿={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|en|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|lt=Hachiman-zukuri|1=Hachiman-zukuri}}銅板葺 | festival=1st Sunday of October | 地図 = {{location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Rokuon Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=200|caption=}} | prefecture={{ill|Iwata|WD=Q386651|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} | coordinates={{Coord|34.70886111111111|N|137.85858333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | architecture_style={{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} }} '''Rokuon Shrine''' (鹿苑神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Ninomiya, {{ill|Iwata|en|Iwata|WD=Q411494|lt=Iwata|1=Iwata}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|en|Shizuoka Prefecture|WD=Q131320|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, the second shrine (Ninomiya) of {{ill|Tōtōmi Province|en|Tōtōmi Province|WD=Q1048864|lt=Tōtōmi Province|1=Tōtōmi Province}}, and was previously Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the old shrine Ranking system. To the north, it borders Renpuku-ji Temple, and it is situated southeast of {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} and south of the Yokosuka Highway. The shrine crest is the "Sixteen Chrysanthemums." Until the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Takane Myōjinja" and enshrined {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|en|Ajisukitakahikone|WD=Q506610|lt=Takahikone no Mikoto|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}. == History == The shrine is identified with the {{ill|Kashikojinja|ja|鹿神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashikojinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} mentioned as a minor shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for Iwata District. The deity of Rokuon Shrine was granted the Junior Fifth Rank in {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō (Japan)|1=Kashō}} 3 (850 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}』 嘉祥3rd year {{ill|July 11|en|July 11|WD=Q2701|lt=7月11日 (旧暦)|1=July 11}}（850th year 8月25日）条 and was further elevated to the Junior Fourth Rank in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|1=Zhenguan}} 2 (860 CE)『{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}』 貞観2nd year {{ill|1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja|正月27日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1月27日 (旧暦)|1=January 27 (Lunisolar)|2=ja|3=1月27日 (旧暦)|4=zh|5=正月廿七|6=ko|7=음력 1월 27일|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（860th year 2月26日）条. It was originally located in Yorigo, Sanka District (modern-day Haruno, {{ill|Tenryū-ku|en|Tenryū-ku|WD=Q1131460|lt=Tenryū-ku|1=Tenryū-ku}}, {{ill|Hamamatsu|en|Hamamatsu|WD=Q185125|lt=Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu}}), near the {{ill|Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja|正国六音大菩薩社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikoku Rokuon Daibosatsu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}『遠江国風土記伝』. During the {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gangyō era|1=Gangyō}} 5 (881 CE), the shrine was moved near the provincial capital of Tōtōmi when Sanka District was separated from Iwata District. By the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was referred to as Ninomiya. Records from {{ill|Bun'ei|en|Bun'ei|WD=Q1010103|lt=Bunei era|1=Bun'ei}} 5 (1268 CE) mention shrine lands under "Tōtōmi Province's Ninomiya Domain."『基重言上状』 During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine served as the [[sōchinju]] for 14 villages, including Ninomiya Village. In the 9th year of {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna (Japan)|1=Yuanhe}} (1623 CE), the shrine buildings were rebuilt, as indicated by surviving {{ill|Munafuda|lt=Munafuda|lt=Mune-fuda|ja|棟札|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mune-fuda|qq=}}. In the first year of {{ill|Keian|en|Keian|WD=Q1146135|lt=Keian era|1=Keian}} (1648 CE), the shrine was granted 23 koku of land. == Grounds == File:Rokuon-jinja, zenkei.jpg|View of the shrine grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (Honden) File:Rokuon-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Shrine within the grounds File:Rokuon-jinja, entrance.jpg|Torii at the entrance File:Rokuon-jinja, torii.jpg|Additional torii == Access == * A 10-minute walk from {{ill|Iwata Station|en|Iwata Station|WD=Q6100757|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * "Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei" (Online Edition), Shogakukan (based on {{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} by Heibonsha, 1979-2002). == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Rokuon-jinja}} {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|鹿苑神社|version=102047584|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿苑神社]] {{Commons category|Rokuon-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11677253}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwata, Shizuoka]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Iwata]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tōtōmi Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
